page: Microsoft Azure F16-4amds_v7 is a memory-intensive server instance powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor. It features 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 32.0 GB per core. Local storage is provided via a bundled 1890 GB disk, and the instance supports Premium Storage. No GPUs are included, and there are zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core capabilities alongside moderate multi-core scaling. In stress-ng testing, single-core CPU performance reached 3772.48, placing it in the top 10% tier, while multi-core performance scored 15055.21, aligning with the middle 50% average. The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 (VNNI and BF16) and cryptography standards like AES and SHA_NI. For language model workloads, the server achieves a top-tier text generation speed of 357.27 tokens per second on a small 135M model, though prompt processing for larger 7B models drops to a weaker 30.84 tokens per second. Static web serving throughput is measured at an average 10.66 GB/sec.

The qualitative cost profile of this instance is defined by its high memory density relative to the compute allocation. With 128.0 GB of RAM paired with only 4 vCPUs, it offers resource efficiency for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel CPU scaling. This makes the server appropriate for in-memory databases, caching layers, and memory-heavy data processing tasks. However, the limited core count presents a tradeoff for highly parallelized compute tasks, where multi-core performance remains average.
description: Microsoft Azure F16-4amds_v7 is a memory-intensive server configured with 4 dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 vCPUs running at 4.5 GHz and 128.0 GB of RAM. This configuration yields a high memory-to-core ratio of 32.0 GB per core, supported by a bundled 1890 GB local storage disk with Premium Storage capability. Benchmark data shows top-tier single-core CPU performance of 3772.48 in stress-ng, while multi-core performance remains in the average tier at 15055.21. The instance excels in single-threaded tasks and small-model text generation, reaching 357.27 tokens per second, but exhibits weaker prompt processing speeds for larger models. Lacking GPU acceleration, this server is optimized for memory-heavy, low-core workloads such as caching layers, relational databases, and in-memory data analytics where memory capacity is prioritized over high parallel compute scaling.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F16-4amds_v7 is a memory-optimized server featuring 4 AMD EPYC vCPUs at 4.5 GHz, 128 GB RAM, and 1890 GB of local storage, delivering top-tier single-core CPU performance.

tagline: A memory-intensive x86_64 platform featuring high single-core clock speeds, massive RAM-to-CPU density, and bundled local storage.

128.0 GB of RAM providing a high 32.0 GB per core memory density
